message:
fix(index): populate actual index path in WAL IndexCorrupted error

The SearchError::IndexCorrupted variant produced when a WAL index is
out of bounds was constructed with `path: PathBuf::new()`, yielding an
empty path in the error message. This made it difficult to diagnose
which index file was corrupted when multiple indices are in use,
because the error gave no indication of the affected path.

Replace `PathBuf::new()` with `self.path.clone()` so the error now
carries the real filesystem path of the VectorIndex that encountered
the corruption. This provides operators with the information they need
to locate and inspect the problematic index without additional
guesswork.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -603,7 +603,7 @@ impl VectorIndex {
             .wal_entries
             .get(wal_idx)
             .ok_or_else(|| SearchError::IndexCorrupted {
-                path: PathBuf::new(),
+                path: self.path.clone(),
                 detail: format!(
                     "WAL index {} out of bounds (wal_entries.len() = {})",
                     wal_idx,
